title: US Medicare names Eli Lilly, Pfizer and AbbVie drugs for next Medicare price talks
summary: The U.S. Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services has identified a new set of branded drugs from major pharmaceutical companies for the upcoming third cycle of the Medicare Drug Price Negotiation Program. This selection includes products from Eli Lilly, Pfizer, and AbbVie, and notably features the first products to be reimbursed under Medicare Part B, expanding the scope of the negotiation program.

title: US Medicare names Eli Lilly, Pfizer and AbbVie drugs for next Medicare price talks
summary: The U.S. Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services has announced a new group of branded drugs from major pharmaceutical companies for the upcoming third cycle of the Medicare Drug Price Negotiation Program. This selection includes products from Eli Lilly, Pfizer, and AbbVie, and notably features the first products reimbursed under Medicare Part B. These drugs will be subject to price negotiations with Medicare.

title: US names Eli Lilly, Pfizer and AbbVie drugs for next Medicare price talks
summary: The U.S. government announced 15 new drugs targeted for Medicare price negotiations for 2028, including treatments from Gilead Sciences, Pfizer, Eli Lilly, and AbbVie. This is the third round of negotiations under the Inflation Reduction Act, aiming to reduce drug costs for Medicare beneficiaries. Despite industry opposition, analysts predict manageable impacts and the Medicare program anticipates significant savings from these negotiations.

title: CRISPR Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CRSP) Insider Purchases $49,703.68 in Stock
summary: CRISPR Therapeutics insider Naimish Patel recently acquired 1,508 shares of the company's stock, totaling $49,703.68. The transaction increases Patel's ownership to 1,508 shares. The company, which has a market cap of $5.17 billion, reported quarterly EPS of ($1.17) and revenue of $0.89 million, and it holds a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with an average target price of $68.15.

title: Pfizer Reshapes Pipeline With Novavax Deal And ViiV Exit
summary: Pfizer has made strategic moves agreeing to license Novavax's Matrix-M adjuvant technology and exiting its stake in the ViiV Healthcare joint venture. These actions signal Pfizer's intent to rebalance its pipeline towards new vaccine opportunities and away from its prior HIV treatment involvement, responding to post-pandemic demand and upcoming patent expirations. Investors are now keen to observe how effectively these shifts will translate into new opportunities and how capital will be reallocated.

title: Rep. J. French Hill Sells Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:KD) Shares
summary: Representative J. French Hill sold a stake in Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. worth between $1,001 and $15,000 on December 31st from his HILL 1998 TRUST. Concurrently, he divested larger amounts from Pfizer, SFL, and Solventum. Kyndryl recently traded near $24.48, and analysts currently rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $38.

title: OKYO Pharma Announces Successful Type C Meeting with the FDA
summary: OKYO Pharma announced a successful Type C meeting with the FDA regarding its Phase 2b/3 clinical trial for urcosimod, aimed at treating neuropathic corneal pain (NCP). The FDA confirmed the trial design, primary endpoint, and sample size, largely de-risking the program. OKYO Pharma plans to initiate the 120-patient Phase 2b/3 study in the first half of this year, addressing a significant unmet medical need for NCP patients.

title: CMS Names Pfizer, Eli Lilly, and AbbVie Drugs for Third Medicare Price Talks
summary: The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) has released a new list of 15 branded medications, targeting drugs from Pfizer, Eli Lilly, and AbbVie, among others, for its third round of Medicare drug price negotiations. These negotiations are part of the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022, aimed at lowering drug costs for Medicare beneficiaries. The selected drugs accounted for $27 billion in spending and are approved by the FDA for at least seven to eleven years without generic alternatives.

title: Biosimilars Market Huge Growth in Future Scope 2026-2033 | Amgen Inc., Pfizer Inc., Samsung Bioepis Co., Ltd.
summary: A new report by Coherent Market Insights forecasts significant growth for the Biosimilars Market between 2026 and 2033, driven by increasing demand, expanding applications, and technological advancements. The study offers a comprehensive overview of market size, revenue trends, and key growth factors, providing actionable insights for businesses. Major players identified include Amgen Inc., Pfizer Inc., and Samsung Bioepis Co., Ltd.

title: Incredible Growth of Recombinant Dna Technology Market
summary: The Recombinant DNA Technology Market is experiencing rapid growth, driven by innovation, increased investment, and shifting regional dynamics. A new study from Coherent Market Insights projects significant opportunities from 2026 to 2033, offering insights into market segmentation, competitive landscapes, and strategic recommendations for decision-makers. Key players include Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc., Biogen Inc., and Sanofi, with market segmentation covering product types like recombinant protein drugs and cell and gene therapy, and applications in therapeutics, agriculture, and research.

title: RNA-Targeted Small Molecule Drugs Market Is Going to Boom |•
summary: The report "RNA-Targeted Small Molecule Drugs Market" provides a comprehensive analysis of the global market from 2026 to 2033. It includes insights into market growth, consumption, size, revenue, share, trends, and cost structures, covering key players like Ionis Pharmaceuticals, Moderna, and Alnylam Pharmaceuticals. The study segments the market by type (Oncology, Infectious Diseases, etc.) and applications (Cancer therapy, Viral infection treatment, etc.), addressing market drivers, challenges, and opportunities across various geographic regions.

title: Jupiter Neurosciences launches support initiative for GLP-1 users
summary: Jupiter Neurosciences (NASDAQ:JUNS) has launched a new initiative to support individuals using GLP-1-based weight loss therapies through its Nugevia consumer longevity product line. The company aims to address biological challenges like mitochondrial energy demands, lean tissue preservation, and neuro-gut inflammatory balance associated with GLP-1 induced weight loss, including the "Ozempic face" phenomenon. This initiative aligns with their strategy combining a pharmaceutical pipeline with a direct-to-consumer longevity business, utilizing their proprietary JOTROL formulation.

title: Roche Makes Obesity Splash, Moderna Cans Vaccine Trials, Sarepta’s New Data, More
summary: Roche announced positive Phase II results for its obesity candidate CT-388, which achieved 22.5% weight loss, comparable to Eli Lilly's Zepbound. Meanwhile, Moderna halted late-stage vaccine trials for infectious diseases, citing challenges from Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy's anti-vaccine policies. Sarepta also released new data for its Duchenne muscular dystrophy gene therapy, Elevidys.

title: AT&T Inc (NYSE:T) Beats Q4 Earnings, Unveils $45 Billion Capital Return Plan
summary: AT&T (NYSE:T) reported Q4 2025 earnings that beat analyst expectations on profit, despite a narrow miss on revenue. The company announced a substantial $45 billion capital return plan for shareholders between 2026 and 2028, driven by strong performance in its fiber and 5G businesses and a confident long-term outlook through 2028. This news led to a positive pre-market reaction, with investors focusing on the company's profitability and commitment to shareholder returns.

title: Utility behind NYC’s lights hikes payout for 52nd year in a row
summary: Consolidated Edison (NYSE: ED) has declared a quarterly common stock dividend of $0.8875 per share, representing its 52nd consecutive annual dividend increase. This raises the annualized payout to $3.55 per share, an increase of $0.15. The company maintains a target dividend payout ratio of 55%-65% of adjusted earnings, emphasizing continued shareholder returns and commitment to service reliability during the clean energy transition.

title: FDA approves repeat treatment protocol for Glaukos’ iDose TR
summary: The FDA has approved a labeling supplement for Glaukos Corporation's iDose TR glaucoma treatment, permitting its re-administration in patients with healthy corneas. This approval is supported by clinical evidence showing the safety of repeat use and provides physicians with greater flexibility in managing glaucoma patients. Glaukos has also shown strong financial performance with significant revenue growth and positive analyst ratings.

title: Notable ETF Outflow Detected - SPYX, PFE, NEM, MDT
summary: The SPDR S&P 500 Fossil Fuel Reserves Free ETF (SPYX) experienced a notable outflow of approximately $144.1 million, a 5.7% decrease week over week. This outflow suggests a reduction in investor demand for SPYX units, potentially impacting its underlying holdings like Pfizer Inc (PFE), Newmont Corp (NEM), and Medtronic PLC (MDT). The article also provides a chart showing SPYX's one-year price performance relative to its 200-day moving average.

title: Bleeding Disorders Treatment Global Market Review 2021-2025 and Forecast 2026-2031 Featuring Profiles of Takeda Pharmaceutical Co., Roche, Novo Nordisk, Pfizer, Bayer and More
summary: The global bleeding disorders treatment market is forecasted to grow from $17.81 billion in 2025 to $29.82 billion by 2031, driven by advancements in gene therapies and extended-half-life products. While growth is robust, high treatment costs remain a significant challenge, particularly in low-income regions, hindering widespread adoption and creating global healthcare disparities. Key trends include the emergence of novel rebalancing agents for inhibitor management and the development of CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing applications, offering potential cures and improved prophylactic care.

title: Intellia Therapeutics to resume dosing of gene therapy in Phase III trial
summary: Intellia Therapeutics has received FDA approval to resume patient enrollment and dosing in its MAGNITUDE-2 Phase III trial for hereditary transthyretin amyloidosis with polyneuropathy (ATTRv-PN), after a clinical hold was lifted. The hold was initially placed following the death of a patient in a related trial, MAGNITUDE, which remains on hold. Intellia's stock price increased following the announcement, and the company has agreed to modifications including enhanced safety monitoring and increased target enrollment for MAGNITUDE-2.

title: AMGEN ANNOUNCES WEBCAST OF 2025 FOURTH QUARTER AND FULL YEAR FINANCIAL RESULTS
summary: Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) has announced that it will report its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results on Tuesday, February 3, 2026, after the U.S. market closes. A conference call with management, led by chairman and CEO Robert A. Bradway, will follow at 4:30 p.m. ET, and a live audio webcast will be accessible on Amgen's Investor Relations site, with a replay available for at least 90 days. Historically, similar webcast announcements have resulted in modest stock price movements, while actual earnings releases have sometimes shown divergence from strong reported metrics due to high investor expectations.

title: Nurix Therapeutics, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
summary: Nurix Therapeutics, Inc. has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing a net loss of $(264.46) million due to increased R&D expenses. The company is advancing a clinical pipeline with three drug candidates and leveraging its DEL-AI platform for drug discovery, supported by collaborations with Gilead, Sanofi, and Pfizer. Nurix faces significant financial and operational risks, including the need for substantial additional funding, but holds a strong intellectual property position.

title: Nurix Therapeutics Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results and Provides a Corporate Update
summary: Nurix Therapeutics announced its Q4 and fiscal year 2025 financial results, highlighting significant progress in its clinical programs, particularly the initiation of the DAYBreak™ registrational program for bexobrutideg in relapsed/refractory chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL). The company reported cash and marketable securities of $592.9 million and detailed clinical data showing the efficacy and durability of bexobrutideg, as well as advancements in its IRAK4 degrader and CBL-B inhibitor programs. Nurix plans to initiate a global randomized confirmatory Phase 3 trial for bexobrutideg in early 2026.

title: American Rebel announces 1-for-20 reverse stock split effective Feb 2
summary: American Rebel Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:AREB) will implement a 1-for-20 reverse stock split and publicly traded warrants, effective February 2, 2026, aiming to meet Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement and enhance stock marketability. The company, which operates with significant debt and weak financial health, will reduce its outstanding shares and has also recently converted $2.05 million of accrued obligations into equity and secured new loan agreements.

title: Rezdiffra Sales Milestone and Pfizer DGAT-2 Deal Might Change The Case For Investing In Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL)
summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals' liver disease drug Rezdiffra has achieved over US$1.00 billion in annualized sales and treated over 29,500 patients. The company further strengthened its MASH treatment pipeline by signing an exclusive global license agreement with Pfizer for ervogastat, a clinical-stage DGAT-2 inhibitor. This strategic move aims to build a broader liver disease treatment franchise, potentially altering Madrigal's investment narrative and future growth prospects.

title: Bank of America Securities Remains a Buy on BioNTech SE (BNTX)
summary: Bank of America Securities analyst Tazeen Ahmad maintained a Buy rating on BioNTech SE (BNTX) with a price target of $134.00, citing the company's financial performance. This positive outlook comes despite negative corporate insider sentiment, indicated by increased insider selling. Other analysts from Morgan Stanley also issued a Buy, while another reiterated a Hold rating for BNTX.

title: Toyota and Georg Fischer HR veteran to lead BioNTech's global people strategy
summary: BioNTech has appointed Kylie Jimenez to its Management Board as Chief People Officer, a newly created role effective March 1, 2026. Jimenez, with over 20 years of global HR experience from companies like Georg Fischer and Toyota, will lead BioNTech's people strategy to support its goal of becoming a multi-product oncology company by 2030. Her responsibilities will include talent attraction, development, retention, and fostering an inclusive culture at the company's Mainz, Germany headquarters.

title: Pfizer's Revenue Rebound Potential is Significant
summary: Pfizer is facing a projected revenue decline in 2025 as it struggles to recover from record COVID-19 vaccine sales and diversify its drug pipeline. However, the company has significant growth potential through new oncology drugs expected by 2030 and its entry into the weight-loss market via the Metsera acquisition. Recent acquisitions like Arena Pharmaceuticals and Seagen further strengthen its future growth prospects, though many new drugs will not complete testing until after 2030.

title: Jim Cramer is Optimistic About Novartis' (NVS) Cancer Drug
summary: Jim Cramer is optimistic about Novartis' (NVS) cancer drug Pluvicto, despite some analysts having reservations about the company's growth projections and the impact of legacy products. Novartis shares have seen a 44% increase over the past year. Cramer's positive outlook on Pluvicto aligns with his previous optimism about cancer treatments from other pharmaceutical companies.

title: Is There Now An Opportunity In Pfizer (PFE) After Recent Pandemic Demand Reset?
summary: Pfizer's stock appears undervalued according to Simply Wall St's analysis, with a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model suggesting a 56.8% discount from its intrinsic value and its P/E ratio being lower than both the industry and its estimated Fair Ratio. The company's recent repositioning post-pandemic demand and its product pipeline are key factors influencing investor perception, making it a potential opportunity for long-term investors. Simply Wall St encourages investors to use its "Narratives" tool to customize their valuation based on their own outlook for the company's future financials.

title: Rezdiffra Sales Milestone and Pfizer DGAT-2 Deal Might Change The Case For Investing In Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL)
summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals' liver disease drug, Rezdiffra, has achieved over US$1.00 billion in annualized sales and treated over 29,500 patients. The company also expanded its MASH treatment pipeline by licensing ervogastat, a DGAT-2 inhibitor, from Pfizer. This dual strategy of strong commercial uptake for Rezdiffra and pipeline expansion aims to build a broader franchise in liver disease treatments, although execution risks and valuation concerns remain.

title: Pfizer Inks $530M Deal for Novavax Matrix-M Adjuvant Technology to Boost Vaccine Pipeline
summary: Pfizer has finalized a $530 million non-exclusive licensing agreement with Novavax for its Matrix-M adjuvant technology. This technology, designed to enhance vaccine immune responses, will be used by Pfizer for up to two infectious diseases, with Novavax supplying the adjuvant. The article also highlights positive results from Pfizer's BREAKWATER trial for metastatic colorectal cancer, showing increased response rates with the BRAFTOVI regimen.

title: Ahead of Earnings, Is Novo Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Novo Nordisk is set to release its full-year earnings for 2025 on Feb. 4, with investors keenly watching the U.S. launch of the Wegovy pill, 2026 guidance, and the ongoing rivalry with Eli Lilly. Morningstar has a fair value estimate of DKK 423.00 for NVO, maintaining a "Wide" economic moat rating and a "High" uncertainty rating due to factors like drug pricing pressures and competition. The article highlights key aspects such as the impact of the Wegovy pill's performance against Lilly's oral competitor, the strategic battle over drug efficacy and market share, and potential acquisitions for growth.

title: Pfizer Inks $530M Deal for Novavax Matrix-M Adjuvant Technology to Boost Vaccine Pipeline
summary: Pfizer has secured a non-exclusive licensing agreement with Novavax for its Matrix-M adjuvant technology through a $530 million deal, aimed at enhancing immune responses for up to two infectious diseases. While Pfizer will manage development and commercialization, Novavax will supply the adjuvant. This news follows Pfizer's positive clinical trial results for its BRAFTOVI regimen in metastatic colorectal cancer, showcasing improved response rates.

title: Fractyl Health Announces Compelling 6-Month Randomized REMAIN-1 Midpoint Data Showing Durable Weight Maintenance with Revita® After GLP-1 Discontinuation
summary: Fractyl Health announced positive 6-month data from the REMAIN-1 Midpoint Cohort study, showing that its Revita® treatment led to durable weight maintenance, improved cardiometabolic profiles, and reduced food cravings in patients after discontinuing GLP-1 drugs. The study demonstrated significantly less weight regain with Revita compared to sham treatment, especially in patients with higher GLP-1-associated weight loss. Fractyl Health is seeking FDA feedback for reclassification of Revita under the De Novo pathway and anticipates pivotal study results and potential FDA filing in H2 2026, positioning Revita as a promising therapy for post-GLP-1 weight maintenance.

title: Lilly, Repertoire enter up to $1.93 billion deal to develop autoimmune therapies
summary: Eli Lilly has partnered with Repertoire Immune Medicines in a deal potentially worth up to $1.93 billion to develop therapies for autoimmune diseases. Repertoire will receive an $85 million upfront payment and up to $1.84 billion in development and commercial milestones, plus royalties. The collaboration aims to develop treatments that restore the immune system and achieve durable remission without generalized immune suppression, utilizing Repertoire's Decode platform.

title: FDA accepts Summit Therapeutics BLA for lung cancer drug ivonescimab
summary: The FDA has accepted Summit Therapeutics' Biologics License Application (BLA) for ivonescimab, a lung cancer drug, in combination with chemotherapy for EGFR-mutated non-small cell lung cancer patients. A Prescription Drug User Fee Act goal action date of November 14, 2026, has been set. The submission is based on positive Phase III HARMONi trial results, and the drug has already received marketing authorization in China.

title: Unicycive Therapeutics Announces FDA Acceptance Of Oxylanthanum Carbonate (OLC) New Drug Application (NDA) Resubmission
summary: Unicycive Therapeutics announced that the FDA has accepted the resubmission of its New Drug Application (NDA) for Oxylanthanum Carbonate (OLC). This significant step moves OLC closer to potential market approval. The news was reported by Reuters and involves the company's stock symbol UNCY.

title: Ruffer LLP Takes $12.11 Million Position in Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. $REGN
summary: Ruffer LLP recently acquired a new position in Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ:REGN) during the third quarter, purchasing 21,540 shares valued at approximately $12.11 million. This move is part of a broader trend of institutional investors modifying their holdings in the biopharmaceutical company, which now has 83.31% institutional ownership. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for REGN, with an average price target of $784.77, while company insiders have recently sold shares.

title: Eli Lilly, BioAge chase NLRP3 inhibitors with ‘pipeline in a pill’ potential
summary: Both Eli Lilly and BioAge Labs are actively developing NLRP3 inhibitors, recognizing their potential as a "pipeline in a pill" to treat a wide range of inflammation-driven diseases, including cardiovascular, neurodegenerative, and metabolic disorders. Eli Lilly recently acquired Ventyx Biosciences for $1.2 billion for its NLRP3 inhibitor pipeline, while BioAge's oral inhibitor, BGE-102, showed promising results in a Phase 1 study for cardiovascular risk factors and plans to expand into ophthalmology. The early-stage development of these drugs aims to offer more convenient oral treatments to address the significant role of inflammation in chronic illnesses.

title: This Beaten-Down Biotech Is Showing Signs of Life. Is It a Buy?
summary: Novavax, a vaccine maker, has seen its shares rise 28% in 2026 after signing a nonexclusive licensing deal with Pfizer for its Matrix-M adjuvant technology, which could yield up to $530 million. Despite this, the biotech's core business in coronavirus vaccines remains uncertain due to inconsistent revenue and changing regulatory recommendations. The article advises against buying Novavax stock due to the uncertainties surrounding its core business, the efficacy of pipeline products using its technology, and setbacks in its internal vaccine development.

title: TD Cowen Adjusts Bristol-Myers Squibb Price Target to $60 From $54, Maintains Hold Rating
summary: TD Cowen has raised its price target for Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) to $60 from $54, while maintaining a Hold rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a moderated outlook, keeping the pharmaceutical giant in a neutral investment position despite the increased target. The report follows other recent analyst adjustments for BMY, indicating varying perspectives on the company's valuation.

title: U.S. vaccine hesitancy could impact investment
summary: Growing vaccine hesitancy in the U.S. is expected to deter venture capital investment in vaccine startups and could reduce valuations for private equity-backed clinical research organizations. This trend is already impacting major pharmaceutical companies like Moderna, which is halting new infectious disease vaccine studies due to concerns about market access and ROI. Despite this, some investors believe the disruption will be temporary, anticipating a shift in public opinion as preventable diseases resurface.

title: Sanofi expects vaccine sales growth to dip amid ‘challenging environment’
summary: Sanofi anticipates a dip in its vaccine sales growth for 2026, following a 2.5% decline in Q4, due to a "challenging environment" stemming from shifting U.S. vaccine policy and declining immunization rates. Executives cited "confusion" and the impact of Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s influence on U.S. vaccine recommendations, which has led to a reduction in recommended childhood vaccinations by the CDC. Despite these challenges, Sanofi plans to continue investing heavily in vaccine development, backed by recent acquisitions.

title: Freedom Capital Markets upgrades Abbott Labs stock rating to Buy on Exact Sciences deal
summary: Freedom Capital Markets has upgraded Abbott Labs (NYSE:ABT) to Buy from Hold, lowering the price target to $120.00 from $130.00, despite Abbott's Q4 results falling below expectations due to weakness in its Nutrition segment. The upgrade is driven by long-term prospects from the anticipated integration of Exact Sciences in Q2 2026, which the firm believes will offset current challenges and lead to conservative revenue growth guidance. Abbott's stock is currently trading near its 52-week low, and InvestingPro analysis suggests it is undervalued.

title: Pfizer CEO says he used ‘emotional blackmail’ to get employees to achieve impossible goals during COVID-19
summary: During the COVID-19 pandemic, Pfizer CEO Albert Bourla admitted to using what he called "emotional blackmail" to motivate his team to achieve seemingly impossible goals, such as rapidly developing and distributing a vaccine. He challenged employees who suggested delays by asking them to consider the number of people who would die during that extra time. While acknowledging some guilt for the intense pressure, Bourla maintained it was necessary to save lives and the economy, ultimately leading to the successful development of the first FDA-approved COVID-19 vaccine and Paxlovid.

title: Xencor (XNCR): BofA Bullish on US Biopharmaceuticals Coverage
summary: Bank of America has raised its price target for Xencor (XNCR) to $20 from $17, maintaining a Neutral rating, as part of its updated coverage on US Biopharmaceuticals. This follows a similar upgrade from Barclays, which increased its price target to $26 from $23. The firm noted a resurgence in the biotech sector due to positive data catalysts, increased M&A activity, better capital access, and limited impact from drug price regulations, although BofA remains cautious about the long-term sustainability of the recovery.

title: NanoViricides Comments on the Need for Broad-Spectrum Antivirals in Light of the Current Influenza Wave - NV-387 is Effective Against H3N2
summary: NanoViricides, Inc. highlights the urgent need for broad-spectrum antiviral drugs, especially given the current "moderately severe" influenza season caused by the H3N2 subclade K variant and the mismatch of seasonal vaccines. Their drug candidate, NV-387, is presented as a revolutionary broad-spectrum antiviral that is effective against various respiratory viruses, including influenza A/H3N2, coronaviruses, and RSV, by targeting a host-side feature (heparan sulfate) that viruses cannot easily escape. The company is planning a Phase II clinical trial for NV-387 as a first-line therapy for acute or severe acute respiratory infections.

title: Kalaris to present phase 1 data of novel eye therapy at Macula Society
summary: Kalaris Therapeutics will present Phase 1 clinical data for its novel eye therapy, TH103, at the 49th Annual Meeting of the Macula Society. The therapy is designed for retinal diseases, and recent positive early data from its Phase 1a trial showed significant improvements in visual acuity and anatomical features, leading to increased analyst confidence and a successful $50 million private placement.

title: Madrigal Weighs Pfizer MASH License And Insider Selling In Growth Plan
summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals entered an exclusive global license agreement with Pfizer for the MASH candidate ervogastat, expanding its treatment pipeline for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis. This deal, aimed at supporting combination therapies with its existing drug Rezdiffra, occurred around the same time as significant share sales by Madrigal insiders, including the CEO and board members. The insider selling may prompt investors to consider execution risks, cash burn, and competition, even as the company strengthens its MASH portfolio and commercial footing.

title: Immutep banks A$30m now, chases A$528m in new efti cancer deal
summary: Immutep (NASDAQ: IMMP / ASX: IMM) reported its Q2 FY26 operational and clinical progress, highlighted by a new strategic collaboration with Dr. Reddy's for its efti cancer deal outside key markets. This partnership includes an upfront payment of approximately A$30.2 million and potential milestone payments up to A$528.4 million, plus royalties. The company also announced strong progress in multiple clinical trials for various cancers and autoimmune diseases, alongside a robust pro-forma cash position of A$129.3 million, extending its financial runway into Q2 CY2027.

title: Madrigal Weighs Pfizer MASH License And Insider Selling In Growth Plan
summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals entered an exclusive global license agreement with Pfizer for the MASH candidate ervogastat, expanding its focus in metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis. This strategic move occurred alongside stock sales by multiple insiders, including the CEO, prompting investors to consider both the company's pipeline development and management's perspective on risk and reward. The new license integrates with Madrigal's existing MASH treatments, such as Rezdiffra, and positions the company for combination therapies while also raising questions about valuation and execution risks due to the insider sales.

title: AstraZeneca Bets $18.5bn On CSPC’s Sustained-Release And AI Peptide Platforms In Weight Management
summary: AstraZeneca has announced a significant $18.5 billion deal with CSPC Pharmaceutical Group, including $1.2 billion upfront, primarily focusing on weight management. This broad collaboration leverages CSPC's sustained-release and AI peptide platforms, marking one of the largest deals in the global weight management sector.

title: Tonix Pharmaceuticals Presented Phase 3 RESILIENT Data on TONMYA™ (Cyclobenzaprine HCl Sublingual Tablets) at the 2026 Non-Opioid Pain Therapeutics Summit
summary: Tonix Pharmaceuticals presented Phase 3 RESILIENT data for TONMYA™ (TNX-102 SL), a sublingual cyclobenzaprine HCl tablet, at the 2026 Non-Opioid Pain Therapeutics Summit. The study showed significant reduction in fibromyalgia pain, improvements in sleep, fatigue, and other symptoms, with TONMYA being well-tolerated. TONMYA, approved by the FDA in August 2025, is designed to bypass first-pass metabolism for optimized parent-drug exposure during sleep, positioning it as a differentiated non-opioid treatment for fibromyalgia.

title: Ruffer LLP Acquires 623,552 Shares of Pfizer Inc. $PFE
summary: Ruffer LLP significantly increased its stake in Pfizer Inc. (NYSE:PFE) by 44.6% in the third quarter, acquiring an additional 623,552 shares and bringing its total holdings to over 2 million shares valued at $51.55 million. This makes Pfizer the 11th largest holding in Ruffer LLP's portfolio, comprising 2.9%. Despite missing revenue estimates, Pfizer beat EPS expectations for the recent quarter and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.43, offering a 6.6% yield.

title: Madrigal Weighs Pfizer MASH License And Insider Selling In Growth Plan
summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL) has entered an exclusive global license agreement with Pfizer for the MASH candidate ervogastat, expanding its pipeline for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis. This move comes alongside recent insider selling by senior leadership, prompting investors to scrutinize both the company's strategic direction and insider activity. The deal aims to complement Madrigal's existing MASH treatment, Rezdiffra, and supports a focus on combination therapies while addressing concerns about valuation and competition.

title: Will BMY's Oncology Collaborations Drive Its Next Growth Phase?
summary: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) is strategically collaborating with companies like Janux Therapeutics and BioNTech to expand its oncology pipeline and portfolio, especially as its legacy products face generic competition. These partnerships involve developing novel treatments like tumor-activated therapeutics and bispecific antibodies, with promising early clinical results in various cancer types. BMY contends with strong competition from Merck and Pfizer in the oncology market, but its shares have outperformed the industry in the last six months and are trading at a discount in terms of valuation.

title: Bank of New York Mellon Corp Lowers Stake in MetLife, Inc. $MET
summary: Bank of New York Mellon Corp significantly reduced its stake in MetLife, Inc. (NYSE:MET) by 39.0% in Q3, selling over 3.1 million shares. Despite this reduction, other major institutional investors like Norges Bank, Pzena Investment Management, Invesco, Nordea, and Amundi increased their holdings in MetLife. Analysts maintain a consensus "Buy" rating for MetLife with an average price target of $95.27, and the company recently outperformed EPS estimates and announced a quarterly dividend.

title: Pfizer Inc (NYSE:PFE) Emerges as a High-Quality Technical Breakout Candidate
summary: Pfizer Inc (NYSE:PFE) is highlighted as a strong technical breakout candidate, exhibiting both a sound technical condition and a high-quality pausing pattern. The stock holds a ChartMill Technical Rating of 7, indicating an uptrend, and a Setup Rating of 10, signaling a clear entry point with defined risk. This analysis suggests PFE is consolidating before a potential upward move, making it an attractive prospect for technical traders.

title: AstraZeneca signs $4.7bn weight loss drug deal with CSPC
summary: AstraZeneca has made a significant move into the lucrative weight loss market by signing a licensing deal worth up to $4.7 billion with China-based CSPC Pharmaceuticals. This agreement grants AstraZeneca access to eight obesity and type 2 diabetes programs, including a once-monthly injectable GLP1RA/GIPR agonist, with an upfront payment of $1.2 billion. The deal strengthens AstraZeneca's pipeline of weight management therapies and underscores its commitment to expanding its presence in China, following a recent announcement of a $15 billion investment in the country by 2030.

title: New York State Common Retirement Fund Sells 7,500 Shares of Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. $REGN
summary: The New York State Common Retirement Fund reduced its stake in Regeneron Pharmaceuticals Inc. by 5.9% during the third quarter, selling 7,500 shares. Other institutional investors have also adjusted their holdings in the biopharmaceutical company. Regeneron Pharmaceuticals reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and has received largely positive ratings from research firms.

title: Wellgistics Health Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results and Provides Business Update
summary: Wellgistics Health reported a net operating loss of $33.878 million in Q3 2025, largely due to increased stock-based compensation. title: Pharming Group receives Complete Response Letter from U.S. FDA for sNDA for Joenja® (leniolisib) in children aged 4 to 11 years with APDS

Pharming plans to work with the FDA to address these issues and proceed with a resubmission, while the approval of Joenja for patients 12 years and older remains unaffected.

title: Pharming Group receives Complete Response Letter from U.S. FDA for sNDA for Joenja® (leniolisib) in children aged 4 to 11 years with APDS

title: Pfizer takes small step toward obesity market, but investors question limited trial results
summary: Pfizer announced preliminary data for its experimental monthly weight-loss injection, showing patients maintained weight loss when switching from weekly to monthly shots. Despite this, investors remain cautious due to limited safety data and strong competition from companies like Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly. Analysts request more comprehensive trial information to fully assess the drug's potential in the competitive obesity market.

title: Pfizer Reports Solid Full-Year 2025 Results And Reaffirms 2026 Guidance
summary: Pfizer reported solid financial results for the full year 2025, with revenues reaching $62.6 billion and adjusted diluted EPS of $3.22. The company reaffirmed its full-year 2026 financial guidance, anticipating revenues between $59.5 to $62.5 billion and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.80 to $3.00, driven by a focus on key therapeutic areas and approximately 20 pivotal study starts planned for 2026. Despite a decline in COVID-19 product revenues, growth in other products like Abrysvo, Eliquis, and the Vyndaqel family contributed to the operational revenue increase of its non-COVID portfolio.

title: Adell Harriman & Carpenter Inc. Purchases 45,578 Shares of Pfizer Inc. $PFE
summary: Adell Harriman & Carpenter Inc. increased its stake in Pfizer Inc. by 21.8%, acquiring 45,578 additional shares, bringing its total to 254,889 shares valued at $6,495,000. This comes as Pfizer reported Q4 earnings that beat estimates with $0.66 EPS and $17.56 billion in revenue, although the company reaffirmed its modest 2026 guidance and faces headwinds from patent cliffs and declining COVID-related sales. Pfizer also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.43, implying a 6.7% yield, raising concerns about its sustainability given the 100% payout ratio.

title: Myocardial Infarction Market Poised for a Boom | Bristol-Myers Squibb Co., Bayer AG, Johnson & Johnson Services Inc.
summary: The Myocardial Infarction market is projected to grow from US$ 1,816.8 million in 2025 to US$ 2,714.8 million by 2032, exhibiting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5.9%. A comprehensive report by Coherent Market Insights details market dynamics, competitive landscapes, and growth opportunities within this sector, featuring key players like Bristol-Myers Squibb Co., Bayer AG, and Johnson & Johnson Services Inc. The study provides segmented analysis by product type, application, end-user, and region, aiming to guide stakeholders, investors, and market participants.

title: Payne Capital LLC Purchases Shares of 10,540 Merck & Co., Inc. $MRK
summary: Payne Capital LLC recently acquired a new stake of 10,540 shares in Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) during the third quarter, valued at approximately $885,000. This comes as several other institutional investors have also adjusted their positions in the pharmaceutical giant. Merck & Co. reported strong Q4 earnings, surpassing analyst estimates, but its 2026 guidance was below expectations due to anticipated patent expirations and increased acquisition spending.

title: Federated Hermes Inc. Cuts Holdings in Krystal Biotech, Inc. $KRYS
summary: Federated Hermes Inc. reduced its stake in Krystal Biotech (NASDAQ:KRYS) by 43.9% in the third quarter, while several other institutional investors increased their positions. This comes as Krystal Biotech reported strong quarterly earnings, significantly beating analyst estimates, and received multiple analyst upgrades, leading to an overall "Buy" consensus with an average price target of $277.89. The company's stock is largely owned by institutional investors and hedge funds, at 86.29%.

title: Boston Scientific announces results for fourth quarter and full year 2025
summary: Boston Scientific (NYSE: BSX) reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, exceeding its guidance goals. The company achieved significant net sales growth across its segments and regions, driven by differentiated innovation and strategic acquisitions. Looking ahead to 2026, Boston Scientific provided optimistic guidance for continued net sales growth and adjusted EPS.

title: Ayrmid Reports Additional New Real-World Data on Motixafortide for Stem Cell Mobilization in Sickle Cell Disease and Beta-Thalassemia
summary: Ayrmid announced new real-world data showing that its drug motixafortide effectively mobilized hematopoietic stem cells (HSCs) in patients with sickle cell disease and beta-thalassemia, facilitating access to gene therapies. The findings, presented at TANDEM 2026, indicated that 73% of patients (11 out of 15) successfully collected enough HSCs for gene therapy manufacturing, with 5 patients already receiving treatment and engrafting properly. Notably, most of these patients had previously failed to mobilize sufficient cells with plerixafor, highlighting motixafortide's potential to overcome a significant barrier to gene therapy access.

title: Pfizer Earnings: Seagen Impairment and Metsera Tolerability Data Incrementally Negative
summary: Morningstar has lowered its fair value estimate for Pfizer (PFE) stock from $34 to $32 per share after the company reported a 2% decline in 2025 revenue and an unexpected impairment charge related to Seagen pipeline programs. Additionally, new data from Pfizer's Metsera obesity program showed a roughly 9% discontinuation rate due to adverse events, raising concerns about the program's long-term potential. Despite these challenges, Morningstar's analysis suggests Pfizer's shares remain undervalued given weak market sentiment.

title: Pfizer (PFE) Announces Positive Phase 2b Results for Obesity Drug Showing 12.30% Weight Loss
summary: Pfizer announced positive topline results from its Phase 2b VESPER-3 study for its obesity drug PF-08653944, showing a statistically significant, placebo-adjusted weight loss of up to 12.3% at 28 weeks. The drug, an injectable GLP-1 receptor agonist, also demonstrated a favorable safety and tolerability profile, with patients continuing to lose weight even after transitioning to monthly dosing. These results bolster Pfizer's extensive obesity development program, with 10 Phase 3 trials expected to begin in 2026 for this candidate, alongside other oral and injectable options.

title: Ashton Thomas Private Wealth LLC Raises Position in RTX Corporation $RTX
summary: Ashton Thomas Private Wealth LLC has increased its stake in RTX Corporation by 9.9% to 78,065 shares, now valued at approximately $13.06 million. This move comes as RTX has secured significant defense contracts, including missile production awards and a substantial radar order, enhancing its revenue visibility. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $198.89, following strong quarterly earnings and positive FY2026 guidance.

title: High-dose nusinersen shows improved outcomes in spinal muscular atrophy
summary: Biogen Inc. announced that its Phase 2/3 DEVOTE study, evaluating a high-dose regimen of nusinersen for spinal muscular atrophy (SMA), demonstrated improved outcomes. The study showed safety and effectiveness across various SMA patients, with a quicker reduction in neurofilament compared to the standard dose. The high-dose regimen is approved in the EU and Japan, and is currently under review by the U.S. FDA.

title: Viking Therapeutics Shares Face Sector-Wide Pressure
summary: Viking Therapeutics' shares are under pressure due to a sector-wide downturn triggered by Novo Nordisk's disappointing 2026 outlook. Investors are now focusing on Viking's upcoming Q4 and full-year 2025 results and management commentary, particularly regarding the VK2735 injectable program’s Phase 3 VANQUISH data timeline and oral formulation strategy, as it seeks to differentiate itself in the competitive obesity treatment market. The company’s ability to provide clear clinical milestones will be crucial for its valuation.

title: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals stock maintains Buy rating at B.Riley on strong Rezdiffra growth
summary: B.Riley has reiterated a Buy rating and $670.00 price target on Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:MDGL) due to the strong performance of its Rezdiffra medication, which showed robust prescription growth and sequential sales expansion in Q4 2025. This performance positions Madrigal to potentially exceed revenue guidance and consensus estimates, despite typical Q1 seasonality. The company has also achieved over $1 billion in annual sales for Rezdiffra by Q3 2025 and expanded its pipeline through an exclusive licensing agreement with Pfizer.

title: Form 424B5 SANGAMO THERAPEUTICS,
summary: Sangamo Therapeutics (SGMO) is offering 35,190,292 shares of common stock, pre-funded warrants to purchase 17,787,033 shares, and accompanying warrants to purchase 52,977,325 shares. The offering is expected to generate approximately $22.9 million in net proceeds, which will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes. The company faces significant financial risks, including substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern, and potential delisting from Nasdaq due to not meeting minimum bid price requirements.

title: Earnings roundup: Amgen vs. FDA, Pfizer’s defense and Merck’s $70B-in-sales plan
summary: This earnings roundup covers Amgen's impressive annual product sales but also its dispute with the FDA over the inflammatory drug Tavneos. The article also details Pfizer's efforts to defend its new obesity drug data against skeptical investors, and Merck's strategic plan to achieve $70 billion in annual sales next decade, moving beyond its blockbuster Keytruda.

title: Eli Lilly Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Eli Lilly discussed its strong Q4 2025 financial results, highlighting a 54% revenue growth driven by key products and an increased market share in incretin analogs. The company provided updates on its pipeline, including the approval of imlunestrant and positive Phase 3 results for Orforglipron in obesity and type 2 diabetes, with one trial showing superiority over oral semaglutide. Lilly also detailed its manufacturing expansion plans and strategic approach to market access and pricing for its new treatments, particularly Orforglipron, emphasizing both direct-to-consumer and traditional payer channels.

title: West Pharmaceutical Services drives innovation in cancer treatment packaging
summary: West Pharmaceutical Services is highlighting its role in advancing cancer treatment through innovative packaging solutions, particularly for radiopharmaceuticals. The company showcases its commitment to improving pharmaceutical packaging to ensure the efficient delivery of these critical treatments. This initiative comes as West Pharmaceutical Services marks World Cancer Day, emphasizing its continuous efforts in the fight against cancer.

title: Cigna settles FTC insulin case, aims to lower drug prices
summary: Cigna's Express Scripts has settled claims with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission regarding its insulin pricing practices, agreeing to changes aimed at lowering costs for patients, insurers, and pharmacies. The 10-year agreement restricts Express Scripts' ability to profit from rebates based on list prices and requires an independent monitor to oversee compliance, with an estimated savings of up to $7 billion for patients. This settlement allows the FTC to resolve a case brought by the former Biden administration, while cases against UnitedHealth Group's Optum and CVS Health Corp's CVS Caremark continue.

title: 13 of the Top 20 Biopharmas Standardize Globally on Veeva Link Key People
summary: Veeva Systems announced that 13 of the top 20 biopharmaceutical companies have standardized on Veeva Link Key People for enhanced customer engagement and AI-driven data. This platform provides critical insights to medical and commercial teams, supports AI initiatives, and has seen significant adoption, including 38 new customers and three top biopharmas making it their global standard within the last year. Recent innovations like AI-powered summaries and the Link Direct Data API further improve utility for the life sciences industry.

title: Express Scripts Reaches Deal With FTC Over Insulin Pricing Practices
summary: Cigna Corp's pharmacy benefits arm, Express Scripts, has reached a settlement with the US Federal Trade Commission regarding allegations that its insulin pricing methods violated antitrust and consumer protection rules. The agreement aims to reduce prescription drug costs for patients, health plans, and independent pharmacies, and includes changes like limiting business practices that critics say drive up prices. The FTC believes these changes could save patients as much as $7 billion over the next decade.

title: Jim Cramer on Merck & Co.: "This Is One of My Favorite Pharmaceutical Stocks"
summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Merck & Co. (NYSE: MRK) as one of his favorite pharmaceutical stocks, noting its solid recent quarter despite a seemingly soft full-year forecast. He explained that the forecast was impacted by one-time acquisition costs, a detail investors understood, leading to the stock's rise. Cramer also praised Merck's "bountiful acquisition spree" and overall strategy, despite its current heavy reliance on Keytruda.

title: Biotech firm Aclaris to appear at Guggenheim, Oppenheimer events
summary: Aclaris Therapeutics (NASDAQ: ACRS) announced its participation in two healthcare conferences in February 2026. CEO Dr. Neal Walker will present at the Guggenheim Emerging Outlook: Biotech Summit on February 12, and President Hugh Davis will speak at the Oppenheimer 36th Annual Healthcare Life Sciences Conference on February 26. Webcasts of both presentations will be available on the company's website for 30 days.

title: Bristol-Myers Squibb Expands RNA And Cell Therapy Pipeline With New Deals
summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) is expanding its pipeline in RNA-based therapies and cell therapies through new strategic initiatives. The company announced the acquisition of Orbital Therapeutics to bolster its RNA portfolio for cancer and autoimmune diseases, and a multi-year partnership with Oxford Biomedica for lentiviral vector manufacturing to support its CAR-T cell therapy programs. These moves indicate a focus on next-generation treatment modalities to address upcoming patent expirations and drive future product launches.

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Healthcare Companies: Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical (RARE) and Pfizer (PFE)
summary: Analysts from Morgan Stanley and Wells Fargo have provided their latest ratings and price targets for Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical (RARE) and Pfizer (PFE). title: Pfizer Earnings: Seagen Impairment and Metsera Tolerability Data Incrementally Negative
summary: Morningstar has lowered its fair value estimate for Pfizer stock from USD 34 to USD 32 per share, following the company's 2025 earnings report. The adjustment is due to an impairment charge partly related to Seagen pipeline programs and mixed tolerability data from the Metsera obesity program MET-097i (PF’3944), leading to reduced long-term growth and margin expectations. Despite these concerns, Pfizer's shares appear undervalued given the current market sentiment.

title: Pfizer Inc. (NYSE:PFE) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Pfizer Inc. (NYSE: PFE) reported its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings, exceeding revenue and adjusted EPS expectations. The company highlighted strong execution, growth in recently launched and acquired products, and strategic advancements in its pipeline, particularly in obesity, oncology, and other therapeutic areas. Pfizer also discussed its significant investments in AI to enhance productivity across R&D, manufacturing, and commercial operations, and reaffirmed its 2026 financial guidance.

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Healthcare Companies: Pfizer (PFE) and Avidity Biosciences (RNA)
summary: Analysts have issued neutral ratings for Pfizer (PFE) and Avidity Biosciences (RNA), indicating neither a bullish nor bearish outlook. UBS analyst Michael Yee maintained a Hold rating on Pfizer with a $25.00 price target, while Citi's Geoff Meacham kept a Hold rating on Avidity Biosciences with a $72.00 price target. The consensus for Pfizer is a Moderate Buy with a 12.5% upside, and for Avidity Biosciences, it is a Hold with a $75.22 price target.

title: Update On ADCs: Sales, Deals, Targets And Approvals
summary: This article provides an update on Antibody-Drug Conjugates (ADCs), covering areas such as sales, deals, targets, and approvals. It is authored by David Wild and published on February 5, 2026.

title: Becoming A Backbone Therapy: Lessons Learned With Actuate Therapeutics
summary: Actuate Therapeutics CEO Dan Schmitt discusses elraglusib's potential as a backbone therapy, highlighting its safety profile and mechanism of action for combination treatments. He explains the trial designs aimed at exploring multiple drug combinations, particularly in metastatic pancreatic cancer and Ewing sarcoma, and shares challenges and lessons learned from running concurrent clinical studies. The article emphasizes the importance of patient experience, regulatory communication, and strategic trial design to establish elraglusib as a significant therapeutic anchor.

title: Interested in our Q4 2025 earnings?
summary: Boston Scientific announced strong financial results for Q4 and the full year 2025, with net sales reaching $5.286 billion for the quarter and $20.074 billion for the year. The company reported significant organic growth across its segments and highlighted key achievements, including FDA and CE mark approvals for new devices, expanded patient access to treatments, and several strategic acquisitions. Chairman and CEO Mike Mahoney expressed optimism for continued growth due to their innovation and team performance.

title: Alvotech Announces Positive Top-Line Results from Pivotal Pharmacokinetic Study for Proposed Biosimilar to Entyvio®
summary: Alvotech has announced positive top-line results from a pivotal pharmacokinetic study for AVT80, its proposed biosimilar to Entyvio® (vedolizumab). The study, which assessed pharmacokinetics, safety, tolerability, and immunogenicity in healthy adult participants, met all primary endpoints, paving the way for regulatory submissions. This success underscores Alvotech's robust platform approach to biosimilar development for both subcutaneous and intravenous formulations of vedolizumab.

title: Pfizer Inc. 2025 Q4 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation (NYSE:PFE)
summary: Pfizer Inc. released its 2025 Q4 earnings call presentation, reporting an EPS of $0.66, beating estimates by $0.09, and revenue of $17.56 billion, surpassing expectations by $729.44 million despite a 1.16% year-over-year decrease. The article highlights the key financial results from the presentation.

title: Arcellx reports D-Domain binder shows no tonic signaling in CAR T therapy
summary: Arcellx, Inc. announced that its D-Domain binder used in anitocabtagene autoleucel (anito-cel) showed no tonic signaling or off-target activity in preclinical studies, suggesting a favorable safety profile for its CAR T therapy. This finding differentiates anito-cel from other BCMA-directed therapies that exhibited off-target activity and tonic signaling. Anito-cel is currently in Phase 2 and Phase 3 studies for multiple myeloma, has received several FDA designations, and is supported by strong analyst ratings.

title: Pfizer Announces 2025 Grant Recipients Driving Innovative Solutions in Cancer Care
summary: Pfizer Canada has announced the 2025 recipients of its oncology grants, investing $900,000 to support innovative, patient-centric solutions for lung, colorectal, prostate, and bladder cancer across Canada. This initiative, part of Pfizer's ongoing commitment since 2021, aims to improve equitable care through projects focused on personalized care, advanced diagnostics, and collaborative treatment strategies, with selected recipients working to address critical gaps in the cancer care pathway.

title: LB Pharmaceuticals lines up $100M for new depression trial
summary: LB Pharmaceuticals announced a private placement worth $100 million through the sale of common stock and pre-funded warrants. The proceeds are earmarked to fund a Phase 2 trial for LB-102, an adjunctive treatment for major depressive disorder, and for general corporate purposes. This financing round saw participation from several institutional investors, and the company has agreed to registration rights for the resale of the securities.

title: Stem Cell Specialist PrimeGen Takes SPAC Track to Nasdaq
summary: PrimeGen US, a stem cell specialist, is going public via a Special Purpose Acquisition Company (SPAC) merger with DT Cloud Star Acquisition Corporation. The deal values PrimeGen at $1.5 billion and will fund its preclinical pipeline of triple-activated mesenchymal stem cells for acute liver injury. This move highlights SPACs as an alternative route to the public market for biotechs, especially those yet to enter clinical trials.

title: Earnings call transcript: IQVIA Q4 2025 beats forecasts, stock drops
summary: IQVIA Holdings Inc. reported strong Q4 2025 financial results, exceeding EPS and revenue forecasts, but its stock dropped 9.02% in pre-market trading due to broader market dynamics. The company highlighted strategic acquisitions and AI advancements as key growth drivers and provided 2026 revenue guidance between $17.15 billion and $17.35 billion, with adjusted EBITDA of $3.975 billion to $4.025 billion. Despite market concerns about AI's disruptive potential, CEO Ari Bousbib emphasized AI as a positive enabler for IQVIA's business, leveraging its proprietary data and deep domain expertise.

title: Bristol-Myers Stock Up as Q4 Earnings and Revenues Beat Estimates
summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) reported better-than-expected Q4 2025 earnings and revenues, with adjusted EPS of $1.26 surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.15, and total revenues of $12.5 billion exceeding estimates. The company's Growth Portfolio, including drugs like Opdivo and Reblozyl, drove a 16% year-over-year revenue increase in this segment, though its Legacy Portfolio saw a 15% decline due to generic competition. BMY also provided a positive 2026 revenue guidance, projecting $46-$47.5 billion, and announced key pipeline updates, including FDA and EU approvals for Breyanzi and a priority review for Opdivo.

title: Bristol-Myers Stock Up as Q4 Earnings and Revenues Beat Estimates
summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter 2025 adjusted earnings per share of $1.26 and total revenues of $12.5 billion, leading to a stock increase. The company's Growth Portfolio, featuring drugs like Opdivo and Reblozyl, drove a 16% year-over-year revenue increase in this segment. Despite strong performance in these new products, the Legacy Portfolio saw a 15% decline due to generic competition, but the company issued an optimistic 2026 revenue guidance of $46-$47.5 billion.

title: Bristol Myers Earnings Reveal Struggling Legacy Drug Business. Why the Stock Is Rising.
summary: Bristol Myers Squibb reported fourth-quarter earnings and revenue that exceeded analyst expectations, despite showing ongoing weakness in its legacy drug business. The company's stock was observed to be rising following the earnings release.

title: Pharmaceutical giant to close 20,000-square-foot facility, lay off 20
summary: Charles River Laboratories is closing its 20,000-square-foot cell therapy facility in Hanover, Maryland, resulting in 20 layoffs. This closure is part of a trend of biotech companies shutting down operations in the region. The facility specialized in gene-edited cell therapies.

title: Big Pharma CEOs set sights on massive growth in years to come
summary: Despite significant headwinds like political pressure, pricing constraints, and patent losses, the CEOs of major pharmaceutical companies like Johnson & Johnson, Pfizer, and Merck & Co. are expressing strong optimism for future revenue growth. They are emphasizing strategies such as M&A, R&D investment, and expansion into lucrative markets like obesity to meet ambitious long-term financial targets and continue fueling the biopharma industry's innovation pipeline.

title: Earnings call transcript: Adaptive Biotechnologies beats Q4 2025 expectations
summary: Adaptive Biotechnologies (ADPT) reported strong Q4 2025 results, significantly beating EPS and revenue expectations. Despite this, the stock declined in aftermarket trading. The company provided optimistic guidance for 2026, forecasting continued revenue growth and aiming for positive adjusted EBITDA by the end of the year, driven by its MRD business and strategic initiatives.

title: Adaptive Biotechnologies soars after beating Q4 expectations
summary: Adaptive Biotechnologies Corporation (NASDAQ:ADPT) saw its shares surge over 6% after reporting significantly better-than-expected fourth-quarter results and full-year performance for 2025. The biotechnology company posted a smaller loss per share and substantially higher revenue, driven largely by its MRD business. It also provided an optimistic revenue forecast for its MRD business in 2026 and highlighted key operational achievements.

title: Adaptive Biotechnologies soars after beating Q4 expectations By Investing.com
summary: Adaptive Biotechnologies (NASDAQ:ADPT) significantly exceeded Q4 analyst expectations, reporting a loss of $0.09 per share against an estimated $0.18 loss, with revenue jumping 51% to $71.7 million, well above the $59.33 million consensus. The company's MRD business, which accounted for 86% of quarterly revenue, grew 54%. Adaptive Biotechnologies also achieved positive Adjusted EBITDA of $4.1 million for the quarter and projects 2026 MRD business revenue between $255 million and $265 million.

title: Pfizer Launches Cost Savings Program on TrumpRx Lowering Drug Costs for Millions of Americans
summary: Pfizer Inc. has launched a new cost savings program on TrumpRx, aiming to make over 30 of its innovative medicines more affordable and accessible to millions of Americans. This initiative is part of Pfizer's Most Favored Nation (MFN) agreement with the U.S. government, offering discounts as high as 85% for uninsured or self-paying patients on a wide range of primary care treatments and select specialty brands. The program seeks to reduce drug costs for U.S. patients while maintaining America's leadership in pharmaceutical innovation, with Pfizer also expanding its direct-to-consumer platform, PfizerForAll.

title: The Truth About Telix Pharmaceuticals: Is This Quiet Biotech About To Go Full Viral?
summary: Telix Pharmaceuticals is gaining attention in biotech circles due to its focus on targeted radiopharmaceuticals for cancer, with existing commercial products generating revenue. The article evaluates Telix's potential against its risks, categorizing it as a high-risk, high-reward investment for tolerant investors due to its innovative product, revenue stream, and market volatility.

summary: AstraZeneca, a British-Swedish multinational pharmaceutical company, has undergone a significant transformation from facing a "patent cliff" a decade ago to becoming a leading force in oncology and rare diseases. title: Pfizer-backed Priovant scores again with fast-rising brepocitinib in midstage study
summary: Priovant Therapeutics, backed by Pfizer, announced positive midstage results for its TYK2/JAK1 inhibitor, brepocitinib, in treating cutaneous sarcoidosis. This marks the first industry-sponsored, placebo-controlled trial to show positive results for the condition, leading Priovant to plan a phase 3 study. Analysts at Leerink Partners called the results "exceptional," with the 45-mg dose achieving a 100% response rate in multiple endpoints.

title: Pfizer Hympavzi label expansion under FDA review (PFE:NYSE)
summary: Pfizer announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has granted priority review for a label expansion seeking a marketing application for its hemophilia therapy, Hympavzi. The New York-based pharmaceutical company had previously submitted a supplemental Biologics License Application for this expansion. This priority review indicates the FDA's intention to take action on the application sooner than standard review times.

title: Arcutis ZORYVE Infant Data Adds New Dimension To Atopic Dermatitis Thesis
summary: Arcutis Biotherapeutics reported positive Phase 2 topline results for its ZORYVE cream in infants with atopic dermatitis, showing a 58% EASI-75 response rate and a mild-to-moderate safety profile. This data expands ZORYVE's potential into a vulnerable pediatric population with limited non-steroidal treatment options. The company plans to file a supplemental New Drug Application in Q2 2026, which could strengthen the ZORYVE franchise despite revenue concentration risks.

title: Earnings call transcript: Immunovant beats Q3 2026 EPS expectations, stock surges
summary: Immunovant Inc. (IMVT) reported a significant beat on its Q3 2026 EPS, coming in at -0.61 against an expected -0.72, leading to an 8.35% premarket stock surge. The company highlighted a strong cash position of $4.5 billion, promising Phase 2 results for Brepocitinib in cutaneous sarcoidosis, and expressed optimism for future commercial launches and pivotal study readouts in 2026 and beyond. Executives emphasized the drug's strong performance in treating orphan inflammatory diseases with high unmet needs.

title: Biogen stock hits 52-week high at 190.34 USD

InvestingPro data suggests the company is undervalued with a P/E ratio of 16.92 and an 8% free cash flow yield, while recent positive news includes successful Phase 2/3 study results for nusinersen and Breakthrough Therapy Designation for litifilimab, despite some analyst price target revisions.

title: Novartis breaks ground on new global Biomedical Research center in San Diego to accelerate drug discovery

If approved, HYMPAVZI would offer a once-weekly subcutaneous injection with minimal preparation and no routine lab monitoring for these patient populations.
